bead.title: bench-pr-A3b: SIGTERM/SIGINT trapping + docker container cleanup + interrupted reports
bead.description:
PROBLEM: A2's per-cell loop does not trap signals. SIGTERM/SIGINT to the runner orphans harbor-runner Docker containers and leaves in-flight cells with no report.

ROOT CAUSE:
  - scripts/benchmark/benchmark (A2): no `trap` for SIGINT/SIGTERM; cells run in the runner's own process group so signals propagate uncontrolled; no docker-label scheme links containers back to cell-ids.

PROPOSED FIX:
  1. `setsid` per cell so each cell process group is independently killable.
  2. Label each `docker run` invocation with `--label fizeau.cell-id=<id>` so containers are findable post-hoc.
  3. `trap _on_signal SIGINT SIGTERM`. Handler: stop scheduling new cells; for each in-flight cell, SIGTERM its process group AND `docker stop` containers matching `--filter label=fizeau.cell-id=<id>`; wait up to 30s; SIGKILL stragglers.
  4. Write final_status="interrupted", process_outcome="killed", no metrics into each in-flight cell's report.json before exit.
  5. Exit non-zero on signal.

NON-SCOPE (preserved from parent):
  - cell-report.schema.json (PR B); bench-set schema (PR C)
  - Node collector/importers (PR D/E); deleting cmd/bench (PR F); docs (PR G)
  - Harbor image, shell adapters, agent-leak fix (A1)
  - Bash runner core, --plan, report.json composition (A2)
  - Parity fixtures + diff.sh (A4)
  - flock / --jobs / in-flight.json (A3a)
  - Cost cap / budget.json (A3c)
  - cmd/bench redirect (A3d)

GOVERNING: plan-2026-05-15-benchmark-runner-simplification. Depends on A2; coordinates with A3a (uses in-flight.json to enumerate cells to clean up).

ACCEPTANCE:
  1. test_runner_signals.sh::test_sigterm_marks_interrupted_and_stops_containers — spawn `./benchmark --profile sindri-lucebox --bench-set tb-2-1-canary --jobs 1 &`; sleep 5; `kill -TERM $!`; wait. Assert runner exit code != 0; in-flight cells' report.json has final_status=="interrupted" and process_outcome=="killed"; `docker ps -q --filter label=fizeau.cell-id` is empty.
  2. test_runner_signals.sh::test_sigint_same_behavior — same as #1 with SIGINT.
  3. test_runner_signals.sh::test_sigkill_after_grace_period — child that ignores SIGTERM is force-killed after 30s grace; report still written.

GATES: `go test ./...` green; `lefthook run pre-commit` green; `bash scripts/benchmark/test/run.sh`.
bead.acceptance: All 3 ACs pass; satisfies parent AC5; go test + lefthook gates green.

events.body: {"decision":"warn","decision_source":"pre_claim_intake","detail":"ambiguous_scope: Well-structured bead with clear acceptance criteria, concrete steps, and explicit non-scope. However, execution depends on two external factors: (1) A2 baseline code availability, which is not explicitly confirmed, and (2) A3a's in-flight.json schema definition, which this bead relies on but does not specify. The AC tests are written assuming in-flight.json exists, but the implementation could proceed with local in-flight cell tracking as a fallback. The core signal handling logic is straightforward and implementable; dependencies are the friction point.","fingerprint":"4a4c61c4c495026c402b5fc3d74cf2535cc4223730d9e55d287891592f499c0c","policy_mode":"warn-only","reason":"readiness_best_effort","rule_id":"pre_claim_intake.readiness_best_effort","suggested_action":"revise the rewrite so it preserves every explicit commitment"}
